candidates.description: The images show a street scene in Panama City with various parked vehicles and pedestrians. The visible road surface shows normal asphalt with some surface wear. There is a drainage gutter/channel visible along the curb edge in images 1 and 2 (Northeast and North views), which is constructed infrastructure, not pavement failure. No actual potholes or missing asphalt are visible in the roadway.

The large dark area visible in the center-bottom of multiple images (especially images 1, 9, and the down view) is a classic Street View near-nadir stitching artifact - a flat, textureless gray/brown smear with no discernible pavement texture, edges, or depth. The surrounding road surface shows normal asphalt in fair condition with some minor surface wear and patching, but no actual potholes or holes with missing asphalt.

A drainage grate/manhole cover is visible in the sidewalk area near the curb in images 2-3. The road surface itself shows normal wear with some rough texture but no actual potholes with missing asphalt. There are construction areas visible with fencing and equipment, but the paved roadway is intact.

The opening visible in images 1, 2, and 9 is a drainage infrastructure element - a storm drain or culvert opening at the curb/gutter junction. It has straight, constructed edges typical of masonry drainage infrastructure, positioned exactly where the sidewalk meets the street. This is not an asphalt pothole but rather a utility opening, possibly with a missing or damaged cover.

The images show a commercial area in Panama City with well-maintained asphalt roads. Image 3 shows drainage grates/storm drains on the road surface which are constructed infrastructure openings, not pavement damage. The road surfaces appear in good condition with normal wear patterns.

The images show a well-maintained urban street in Panama City with good quality asphalt. The large olive/khaki colored areas visible in multiple images (particularly images 1, 2, 7, 8, 9) are the characteristic Google Street View near-nadir stitching artifacts - flat, textureless smears with no discernible pavement detail. The actual visible road surfaces in images 3-6 show clean asphalt with white lane markings, proper drainage infrastructure (metal grates visible in images 4-5), and no potholes or significant damage.

The location shows a commercial/retail area in Panama City near Multiplaza mall. The road surfaces visible are in generally good condition with normal wear. There are drainage channels and utility covers visible along the sidewalks and curb areas, but no potholes in the asphalt roadway itself.

The prominent feature visible in multiple images is a large rectangular storm drain grate at a street corner/curb junction. This is constructed drainage infrastructure with metal grating, not a pothole. The drain has straight manufactured edges and is properly set into the concrete curb area.

Large area of standing water/flooding visible on the roadway and driveway entrance area. The water appears murky brown/green and completely obscures the pavement surface beneath it. No edges, depth, exposed base material, or jagged broken asphalt edges are visible through the water - only the waterline against grass and concrete surfaces can be seen.

The location shows a commercial parking area with visible drainage grates/channels running along the curb edge. Image 2 shows a raised concrete island with traffic cones and utility pole. The yellow drainage grates visible in multiple images are constructed infrastructure, not pavement failure. No potholes or asphalt damage detected.

The downward-facing image (Image 9) shows a large gray-brown smear that is characteristic of Street View near-nadir stitching artifacts - it lacks discernible pavement texture and appears as a flat, textureless area. The horizontal street view images show a commercial urban street in Panama City with parked vehicles, drainage grates along the curb, and generally intact asphalt surface.

The images show a mix of interior office spaces (high-rise building with marble floors, office furniture, workers at desks) and street-level views. The street-level images (Northeast, East, Southwest) show urban streets in Panama City's Obarrio neighborhood with construction areas, parked vehicles, and motorcycles. No potholes or significant road damage visible in the street views - surfaces appear to be worn but intact asphalt and concrete.

The images show a commercial area in Panama City with well-maintained asphalt roads. Image 3 shows drainage grates/storm drains on the road surface which are constructed infrastructure openings, not pavement damage. The road surfaces appear in good condition with normal wear patterns.

The yellow drainage grates visible in multiple images are constructed infrastructure, not pavement failure. No potholes or asphalt damage detected.

The street view images show a commercial/urban area in Panama City with parked vehicles, a parking lot with traffic cones, and retail storefronts including Lenox and Kate Spade. The road surface appears to be standard asphalt in fair condition with normal wear but no visible potholes or significant damage.
